skill.name: trigger-dev
skill.description: Guides Trigger.dev implementation for TypeScript background jobs, scheduled tasks, AI workflows, webhooks, retries, and queue design.
skill.summary: Build reliable Trigger.dev background jobs and async workflows in TypeScript projects.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged duplicate shell-execution and weak-cryptography patterns, but review found no executable code, shell invocation, or cryptographic implementation. The reported backticks are markdown code spans at SKILL.md line 67, and the weak-crypto hits are ordinary prose at lines 3 and 17. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or hidden execution behavior was found in the single reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Spans Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The duplicate external command findings point to SKILL.md line 67, where related skill names appear inside markdown backticks. These backticks are not Ruby or shell execution and do not run commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 67 contains only a markdown sentence listing related skills in inline code formatting. There is no script block, interpreter directive, command substitution, or executable context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Prose
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings point to SKILL.md lines 3 and 17, but those lines contain natural-language description text. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, key handling, or encryption code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is frontmatter and prose about developer experience and task design. The reviewed file does not implement or recommend DES, MD5, SHA1, or other weak cryptographic operations.

content.value_statement: Reliable async work is hard to design when jobs run longer than a request. This skill helps teams build Trigger.dev tasks with retries, schedules, queues, and integration patterns.
actual_capabilities: Explains Trigger.dev task setup for Next.js and TypeScript projects.
Guides AI background jobs that use Trigger.dev integrations and retries.
Covers scheduled triggers, webhook handlers, queues, and batch processing patterns.
Highlights sharp edges such as timeouts, payload serialization, environment sync, and SDK version mismatch.
Recommends task design practices for idempotency, concurrency limits, and logging.
limitations: Does not include runnable source files or packaged Trigger.dev examples.
Does not execute deployments, install packages, or manage cloud settings by itself.
Provides guidance only for patterns named in the skill file.
Requires current Trigger.dev documentation for version-specific API details.

use_cases.title: Ship Async Product Workflows
use_cases.description: Design Trigger.dev tasks for slow operations such as imports, reports, and integration syncs.

use_cases.title: Run AI Jobs Reliably
use_cases.description: Move long-running model workflows out of request paths and add retries, logging, and queue control.

use_cases.title: Review Task Architecture
use_cases.description: Check background job plans for serialization issues, duplicate side effects, timeouts, and concurrency risks.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a First Task
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me design a Trigger.dev task for a Next.js app. Explain the task shape, trigger, payload, retry behavior, and logging plan.

prompt_templates.title: Add a Scheduled Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a scheduled Trigger.dev workflow for this recurring job. Include cron timing, payload design, timeout settings, and failure handling.

prompt_templates.title: Improve an AI Background Job
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my AI background job plan for Trigger.dev. Focus on retries, idempotency, OpenAI integration use, queue limits, and observability.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Production Task System
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this Trigger.dev task architecture for production risks. Identify monolithic tasks, duplicate side effects, unsafe payloads, timeout gaps, and concurrency problems.

output_examples.input: A developer asks how to move report generation out of an API route.
output: A Trigger.dev task boundary with a plain object payload.
A trigger plan that starts the task after the user request succeeds.
Retry, timeout, and logging recommendations for the report workflow.

output_examples.input: An AI team needs a long-running summarization pipeline.
output: A staged workflow plan for document loading, model calls, and result persistence.
Guidance on built-in integrations, automatic retries, and queue limits.
Checks for idempotency and duplicate side effects.

output_examples.input: An engineering lead wants a review of scheduled sync jobs.
output: A risk review covering cron timing, downstream rate limits, payload serialization, and environment variable sync.
Suggested task splits for large batches.
Operational checks for version alignment and observability.

best_practices: Keep tasks focused, observable, and easy to retry safely.
Use plain serializable payloads and sync required environment variables before deployment.
Set explicit timeouts, idempotency keys, and queue concurrency limits for production work.
anti_patterns: Do not place unrelated workflows in one giant task.
Do not ignore built-in integrations when they provide retries and safer defaults.
Do not run side effects without logging, idempotency, or duplicate protection.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps design Trigger.dev background jobs, scheduled tasks, AI workflows, webhook handlers, queues, and batch processes.

faq.question: Is it only for Next.js projects?
faq.answer: The skill mentions Next.js setup, but its task design guidance also applies to TypeScript services that use Trigger.dev.

faq.question: Can it deploy Trigger.dev tasks?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance, but deployment still depends on your project tooling and Trigger.dev configuration.

faq.question: Does it include security guidance?
faq.answer: It covers operational risks such as duplicate side effects, environment sync, payload serialization, timeouts, and concurrency limits.

faq.question: Can it help with AI workflows?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes AI background jobs and Trigger.dev integration patterns for long-running model workflows.

faq.question: Should I check current Trigger.dev docs?
faq.answer: Yes. Use current Trigger.dev documentation for exact SDK APIs, CLI behavior, and cloud configuration details.
